title: AI News Daily — July 17, 2026
summary: The frontier race is being decided on plumbing, not demos. Google scrapped and rebuilt Gemini 3.5 Pro to fix its coding weakness and is targeting launch today; NVIDIA shipped a chart-topping open embedding family; and Fireworks raised $1.5B on the economics of serving open models cheaply. Meanwhile GPT-5.6 Sol posted the first real score on the brutal ARC-AGI-3 reasoning benchmark, and Google folded NotebookLM into the Gemini brand.
